content: Node.js EventEmitter 'error' event special-cases: if an emitter emits 'error' and no listener is attached, Node throws the error as an uncaught exception, crashing the process (documented Node.js behavior, not a bug). This matters for a shared domainEvents bus: any listener registered by notificationService that can throw/reject must catch its own errors internally (log + swallow, or re-emit a non-'error'-named failure event) rather than letting an exception propagate out of the event loop turn -- otherwise a bug in one notification handler could crash the entire server on a wager/market event unrelated to notifications.
